findings.title: Concurrency risk and potential deadlock from Promise.all in interactive transaction
findings.message: Executing database updates concurrently using Promise.all inside a Prisma interactive transaction ($transaction) is a known performance anti-pattern. Because interactive transactions serialize operations on a single database connection, triggering concurrent updates via Promise.all can lead to connection pool starvation, deadlocks, or transaction timeouts.
findings.impact: When rebuilding rule-calibration cards with a large volume of rule feedback, the memory rebuild process can suffer from transactional deadlocks, query timeouts, or database connection exhaustion.
findings.suggestion: Execute the update operations sequentially using a 'for...of' loop inside the transaction instead of using Promise.all.
findings.verification: Rebuild the memory cards on a database populated with a large volume of distinct rule feedback. Verify that executing the updates sequentially completes reliably without transaction deadlocks or database connection starvation.
